diagrams.title: Raster Scan Display Pattern
diagrams.description: Shows the horizontal line-by-line scanning pattern used in raster scan displays. The electron beam sweeps across each row from left to right, then moves down to the next line. This refreshes the entire screen repeatedly.
diagrams.purpose: Explains how raster scan displays work by continuously refreshing the screen row by row, which is the foundation of modern monitors.

diagrams.title: Random Scan vs Raster Scan Comparison
diagrams.description: Side-by-side comparison showing how random scan (vector) displays draw lines by moving the electron beam directly between endpoints, while raster scan draws complete scan lines. Shows the difference in beam movement patterns.
diagrams.purpose: Clarifies the fundamental difference between random scan (older technology) and raster scan (current standard) display methods.

diagrams.title: Raster Display Architecture
diagrams.description: Block diagram showing the components of a raster graphics system including the frame buffer, video controller, digital-to-analog converter, and CRT display. Shows how pixel data flows from memory to screen.
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the complete data flow in raster graphics systems, from storing pixel values in memory to displaying them on screen.
